def compute_factor(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.Series:
    # Sort by (code, date) for per-code rolling operations
    df = df.sort(['code', 'date'])
    
    # Part 1: delta(close, 7)
    d_close = delta(pl.col('close'), periods=7)
    
    # adv20 = 20-day average volume
    adv20 = rolling_mean(pl.col('volume'), window=20)
    
    # volume / adv20
    vol_ratio = pl.col('volume') / adv20
    
    # decay_linear(volume / adv20, 9)
    decay = decay_linear(vol_ratio, window=9)
    
    # rank(decay_linear(...))
    rank_decay = rank(decay).over('date')
    
    # 1 - rank(decay_linear(...))
    one_minus_rank = 1 - rank_decay
    
    # delta(close, 7) * (1 - rank(...))
    product = d_close * one_minus_rank
    
    # rank(product)
    rank_product = rank(product).over('date')
    
    # -1 * rank(product)
    neg_rank = -1 * rank_product
    
    # Part 2: sum(returns, 250)
    sum_returns = rolling_sum(pl.col('returns'), window=250)
    
    # rank(sum(returns, 250))
    rank_sum = rank(sum_returns).over('date')
    
    # 1 + rank(sum(returns, 250))
    one_plus_rank_sum = 1 + rank_sum
    
    # Final factor: (-1 * rank(...)) * (1 + rank(sum(returns, 250)))
    factor = neg_rank * one_plus_rank_sum
    
    return df.select(factor).to_series()
